title: Noccaea microphylla
intro: Noccaea microphylla, also known as Pygmy Penny Cress, is a perennial plant species belonging to the mustard family (Brassicaceae). This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Noccaea microphylla include isothiocyanates, particularly glucoraphanin and its hydrolytic product sulforaphane, which have been shown to have anticancer and antimicrobial properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Noccaea microphylla include dietary supplements, teas, and extracts, which are often used to support overall health and well-being, as well as to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
taxonomy: Noccaea microphylla is classified within the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that also includes genera such as Arabidopsis, Brassica, and Draba. Noccaea microphylla is a member of the genus Noccaea, which is primarily composed of small, perennial herbs native to the Northern Hemisphere. The genus Noccaea is part of the tribe Lepidieae, which is a subtribe within the larger Brassicaceae family. The classification of Noccaea microphylla is further supported by its morphological and molecular characteristics.
